objective: Extended IPF lung evidence subset that keeps live accession-backed transcriptomics cohorts and admits explicit IPF/control subsetting for mixed-diagnosis studies.
inclusion_principles: Prefer lung-tissue IPF versus control cohorts with live accession-backed loaders.
Allow mixed-diagnosis cohorts only when the public sample metadata exposes an explicit IPF versus control split.
Keep this subset separate from the first-wave benchmark core so broader evidence does not silently change benchmark claims.

included_datasets.reason: Direct lung case-control RNA-seq cohort with explicit counts and a live accession-backed loader.
Direct IPF versus control lung RNA-seq cohort from LTRC with a live matrix-backed loader.

included_datasets.control_definition: Healthy donor lungs collected at transplantation
included_datasets.reason: Direct IPF versus healthy-donor lung comparison with a live accession-backed microarray loader.
Public count matrix and sample metadata expose an explicit IPF versus control split inside a broader CHP/IPF/control cohort.

excluded_datasets.reason: Included in the first-wave benchmark core, but current platform mapping remains unresolved for a live loader.
Included in the first-wave benchmark core, but its public supplements are virus-focused rather than a straightforward human gene-count matrix.
Blood cohort with longitudinal follow-up rather than a lung-tissue subset.
Blood PBMC case-control cohort kept separate from the lung-focused extended subset.
Replication cohort without a clean control arm in the accession-level design text.
Blood transcriptome cohort kept separate from lung-focused evidence.
Chronic fibrosing IIP cohort rather than a clean IPF-only case-control series.
Case-count discrepancy between GEO summary and overall design remains unresolved.
Mixed ILD cohort with only a UIP/IPF subset.
IPF with secondary pulmonary hypertension plus a separate PAH arm.
Affected, unaffected, and healthy-control regions are mixed without subgroup counts in the accession text.
Single-cell follow-up dataset reserved for mechanistic localization, not bulk discovery benchmarking.

notes: This is an exploratory PrioriTx subset for broader live transcriptomics evidence and is not the same as the stricter first-wave benchmark core.
